content.text: Human beings are naturally curious about their surroundings from childhood. Science is a way of thinking, observing, and doing things to understand the world and uncover the secrets of the universe.
The foundation of science is 'Curiosity'. It applies to everything from tiny grains of sand to massive mountains, and from cooking in the kitchen to exploring outer space.
Every new discovery is like a piece of a giant jigsaw puzzle. New knowledge often leads to more questions, and sometimes we even need to move pieces if a discovery changes our previous understanding.
Science is not just about memorising facts; it is about following a systematic process to find answers.

rows: Observe something interesting or not understood.
Wonder and think of a question about it.
Test this guess through experiments or more observations.
Analyse results to see if the question is actually answered.

content.title: Example 1
content.description: Suppose your pen stops writing. How would you apply the scientific method to fix it?
content.solution:
1) Observe: The pen is not writing.
2) Question: Why did it stop?
3) Guess: The ink finished.
4) Test: Open the pen and check the refill.
5) Analyse: If empty, replace it; if not, make a new guess (e.g., ink dried).

content.text:
Anyone who follows the scientific method acts like a scientist. For example:
- A cook wondering why dal spilled (Was there too much water?)
- A bicycle repair person finding a tyre leak.
- An electrician checking why a bulb is not working (Is it the bulb or the switch?)

content.title: Example 2
content.description: An electrician is checking a light bulb that won't turn on. What are the two likely guesses he might make to test the problem?
content.solution:
Guess 1: The bulb is fused (he will test by replacing the bulb).
Guess 2: The switch is faulty (he will test by checking the wiring/connection).

content.title: Example 3
content.description: A person cooking dal notices it has spilled out of the pressure cooker. What logical guess can they make to investigate this using the scientific method?
content.solution:
Guess: There was too much water added to the cooker.
Test: Check the water level used compared to previous successful cooking sessions.

content.text: Science is rarely done alone; scientists across the world work in teams. Learning science develops the ability to solve big mysteries of the universe, but the journey of exploration continues throughout life.
To be a wise person, you must be a 'whys' person! Joyful exploration and never-ending questions are the keys to science.

glossary.definition: The desire to know or learn something; the starting point of any scientific inquiry.
A systematic, step-by-step process used to find answers to questions about the world.
The act of noticing something interesting or unusual using our senses.
The collaboration of many scientists working together to solve complex problems.

summary_points: Science is a process of thinking, observing, and doing to understand the world.
Curiosity is the most important element for embarking on a scientific journey.
The scientific method involves observing, questioning, guessing, testing, and analysing.
Everyone acts like a scientist when they use logical steps to solve daily-life problems (e.g., fixing a pen or cooking).
Discoveries are connected like pieces of an unending jigsaw puzzle.
